steps.title: Find the greatest common factor
steps.content: In $2x^{3}+4$, the coefficients are $2$ and $4$, whose greatest common factor is $2$. The variable $x$ appears only in the first term, so it is not part of the common factor.

steps.title: Factor and test each choice
steps.content:
Factor: $2x^{3}+4=2(x^{3}+2)$. Now expand each choice and compare to $2x^{3}+4$:
  • A) $4(x^{3}+4)=4x^{3}+16$. The leading coefficient is $4$, not $2$, so this is not equivalent.
  • B) $4(x^{3}+2)=4x^{3}+8$. The leading coefficient is $4$, not $2$, so this is not equivalent.
  • C) $2(x^{3}+4)=2x^{3}+8$. The constant term is $8$, not $4$, so this is not equivalent.
  • D) $2(x^{3}+2)=2x^{3}+4$. This matches the original expression exactly. That matches choice D — D is correct.
